title: limonium ramosissimum subsp. doerfleri
intro: Limonium ramosissimum subsp. doerfleri, also known as doerfler's sea lavender, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Plumbaginaceae family, characterized by its delicate purple flowers and woody stems. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of doerfler's sea lavender include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress and anxiety, and exhibit antibacterial properties, which can help prevent infections and promote overall well-being. Herbal preparations of doerfler's sea lavender include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to soothe skin irritations, calm respiratory issues, and promote relaxation.
